results.query: Use datocms-setup to configure one blueprint profile and two destination profiles that share the same migrations directory.
Use datocms-setup to add declarative DatoCMS build trigger config plus sync and smoke scripts.
Use datocms-setup to wire granular DatoCMS cache invalidation for this Next.js app with revalidateTag() and persistent tag storage.
Use datocms-setup to install @datocms/cda-client, add the published CDA token placeholder, and scaffold the shared query utility for this Astro project.
Use datocms-setup to add staging and production profiles to datocms.config.json and wire the per-profile token placeholders.
Use datocms-setup to add generate-cma-types, the CMA token env placeholder, and the cma-types.ts output path for this project.
Use datocms-setup to enable stega encoding and add click-to-edit overlays to this Next.js draft site.
Use datocms-setup to install the DatoCMS Contentful import plugin, add env placeholders, and scaffold a local helper.
Use datocms-setup to add enable and disable endpoints, dual-token executeQuery wiring, and the required env placeholders for this Next.js app.
Use datocms-setup to configure gql.tada for typed DatoCMS GraphQL queries in this Next.js project.
Use datocms-setup to add a helper around migrations:new --autogenerate for this repo.
Use datocms-setup to add a local release helper for DatoCMS migrations with maintenance mode and promotion.
Use datocms-setup to scaffold @datocms/cli in this project, create datocms.config.json, add a migrations folder, and wire the baseline scripts.
Use datocms-setup to add live DatoCMS preview updates over SSE to this Next.js draft site.
Use datocms-setup to add a shared Dato image wrapper and patch one real image field in this Next.js project.
Use datocms-setup to add framework-native robots.txt and sitemap routes for this Next.js site with shared public URL mapping.
Use datocms-setup to add a helper that destroys and reforks a DatoCMS sandbox, then reruns migrations in place while I iterate locally.
Use datocms-setup to wire _seoMetaTags, favicon meta tags, and canonical URL helpers into this Next.js project.
Use datocms-setup to provision DatoCMS Site Search, scaffold the least-privilege token env vars, and mount a search route in this Next.js repo.
Use datocms-setup to add a shared renderer and patch one real Structured Text field in this Next.js repo.
Use datocms-setup to add a shared Mux video wrapper and patch one real Dato video field in this Next.js repo.
Use datocms-setup to add a preview-links endpoint, CORS handling, and record-to-URL routing for this Next.js repo.
Use datocms-setup to add a declarative webhook config, a sync helper, and a local authenticated DatoCMS webhook receiver.
Use datocms-setup to install the DatoCMS WordPress import plugin, add env placeholders, and scaffold a local helper.
Use datocms-setup to set up full visual editing for this Next.js site with DatoCMS draft mode, Web Previews, and click-to-edit overlays.
Use datocms-setup to add side-by-side editing in DatoCMS for this Nuxt project.
Use datocms-setup to add website click-to-edit only for this SvelteKit draft site.
Use datocms-setup to set up DatoCMS preview and editor workflows for this Astro project.
Use datocms-setup to configure Site Search for a site with /blog and /docs sections.
Use datocms-setup to wire DatoCMS visual mode into a Vercel project that already has Edit Mode enabled.
Use datocms-setup to set up DatoCMS migrations in a repo with custom staging and production profiles plus an existing migrations directory.
Use datocms-setup to add a release workflow but preserve the current default release profile if one already exists.
Use datocms-setup to install WordPress import support for an existing DatoCMS project and keep destructive options explicit.
Use datocms-setup to add blueprint sync for duplicated projects that need to keep entity IDs aligned.

I need to pull all blog posts from my DatoCMS project and display them on my Next.js site. They have a title, slug, cover image, and a rich text body. Can you write the GraphQL query and the page component?

I need to create a new blog post in DatoCMS programmatically using the CMA client. The model has title (string), slug (slug), body (structured text), and published_date (date) fields. Can you write the script?
I need to create a DatoCMS migration script that adds a new Event model with title, date, location, and description fields. How do I scaffold the migration and run it on my sandbox environment?
I want to build a custom DatoCMS plugin that adds a sidebar panel showing word count, reading time, and SEO score for the current record. It should use renderItemFormSidebar from the plugin SDK.
I need to create 300 product records in DatoCMS programmatically from a CSV file. Each product has a name, price, description, and image URL. Can you write the import script using the CMA client?
How do I query all blog posts from DatoCMS using the Content Delivery API GraphQL endpoint? I need filtering by category and cursor-based pagination.
I want to build a custom field extension plugin for DatoCMS that renders a map picker. The editor clicks on a map to select coordinates. Using the plugin SDK connect() function.
How do I fork my DatoCMS environment, make schema changes via the CMA, and then promote it to primary? I want to do this in a CI pipeline.
I am setting up a Supabase project with a PostgreSQL database. Can you help me write GraphQL queries using Hasura to fetch user profiles with their recent posts?
Can you help me set up a Strapi headless CMS with Next.js? I need to fetch content from Strapi's REST API and display it in my pages.
Use datocms-setup to wire a ContentLink component into my existing Next.js page and patch the current renderer only.
Use datocms-setup to add visual mode for this Next.js repo, but keep realtime off unless it is truly needed.
Use datocms-setup to preserve the existing Vercel website edit overlays unless full DatoCMS side-by-side editing is required.
Use datocms-setup to configure typed GraphQL queries in a repo that already uses GraphQL Code Generator and .graphql documents.
Use datocms-setup to add Site Search for a site that already exposes /blog, /docs, and /help.
Use datocms-setup to patch my existing preview-links route handler only and keep the rest of the setup untouched.
Can you wire @vercel/toolbar into this Next.js repo for Edit Mode overlays?
Use datocms-setup to add a live preview workflow for this Next.js site with draft mode, click-to-edit, side-by-side editing, and instant updates.
Use datocms-setup to replace existing Vercel Content Link with DatoCMS visual editing for this Next.js site.
Use datocms-setup to configure DatoCMS Site Search with separate indexes and search routes for /blog and /docs.
Use datocms-setup to patch one missing ContentLink boundary in the existing Structured Text renderer and leave the rest of the visual editing setup untouched.
Our Vercel project already has working Edit Mode overlays. Explain whether DatoCMS visual editing should replace that or live alongside it.
Use datocms-setup to build the React site-search UI against the existing public search endpoint and current indexes only.
Patch the existing preview-links endpoint so the author model resolves to /authors/[slug]; do not scaffold new preview setup.
Use datocms-setup to add one more itemFormDropdownActions entry to our existing DatoCMS plugin and leave the current UI alone.
